name: Aardvark
description: Aardvarks are a type of anteater totally adapted to feeding on ants and termites. They are equipped with claws for digging into ant and termite lairs. Aardvarks eat insects with a long sticky tongue. They are found only in southern Africa.

name: Alligator & Crocodile
description: Alligators are the largest reptiles in North America. They are dangerous swamp-dwelling carnivores. Their horny scales provide extra protection. Long jaws filled with conical teeth are designed for grasping and tearing apart prey. Crocodiles are basically the same.

name: Apes
description: The great apes are the closest relatives to man in existence. Includes Chimpanzees (Size 9, BIO-E 10), Gorillas (Size 11, BIO-E 0), and Orangutans (Size 8, BIO-E 15). In all cases they already have partial hands, upright stance and human looks. Base stats shown are for Chimpanzee.

name: Armadillo
description: These are naturally armoured mammals. They come in a variety of species that range in size and the quality of armour. The kind described here is the ninebanded armadillo commonly found in the Southern U.S.
length: 2 feet, plus a foot of tail

name: Baboon
description: These animals are powerful ground dwelling tribal monkeys. Mandrills and Geladas should have the same characteristics.

name: Badger
description: Badgers are squat, carnivorous animals who spend most of their time digging into the underground nests of their prey.

name: Bat
description: Bats are flying mammals. Their Sonar sense is particularly useful because they are primarily nocturnal and spend daylight hours in completely dark caves. Most bats are insect and fruit eaters.

name: Bear
description: There are several different kinds of bears. Smallest are the Black Bears (Size 14, BIO-E 5) found all over North America. Grizzly Bears (Size 16, BIO-E 0) are next; even bigger are the Brown Bears of Alaska (Size 17, BIO-E 0), sometimes called Kodiak Bears. The largest are the Polar Bears (Size 18, BIO-E 0). Base stats shown are for Black Bear.

name: Beaver
description: Dam-building rodents with wide, flat tails. They are natural engineers and are very family oriented.

name: Boar
description: Dangerous wild pigs which were imported to the U.S. as a game animal. Boars are quick and dangerous with large, sharp tusks protruding from sides of the mouth.

name: Buffalo
description: These huge grazing animals were virtually exterminated by hunters in the west.

name: Camel
description: These large desert grazing animals have been used in the Middle East as beasts of burden for centuries. In spite of their long contact with man they remain vicious and short tempered. Camels can go for several days without food or water, losing up to 25% of body weight and regaining it all back within minutes of drinking.

name: Canines, Wild
description: Coyotes (Size 5, BIO-E 55) and Wolves (Size 6, BIO-E 50) are carnivores who hunt in highly organized packs. They are intelligent and very social. Base stats shown are for Wolf.
length: Coyote: 4-5 feet long; Wolf: 5-6 feet long

name: Canine: Fox
description: There are two major kinds of foxes in North America — the Red (a furred fox) and the Grey (a haired fox). Both are small, clever carnivores. There is also a white Arctic Fox with the same characteristics.

name: Cat — Domestic
description: There are a wide variety of cat breeds, but all have roughly the same characteristics. Cat characters can have spotted, striped, persian or calico body markings.

name: Cat — Wild
description: Includes the Bobcat (Size 4, BIO-E 65) and Lynx (Size 5, BIO-E 60), both tree-climbing feline hunters. The wild felines are solitary creatures who avoid areas inhabited by man. Ocelots and Servals are similar. Base stats shown are for Bobcat.

name: Cheetah
description: A large, spotted feline that looks somewhat doglike. Cheetahs are the fastest mammals in the world and use their speed for hunting.

name: Chicken
description: Domestic animal bred for food and egg-laying. Chickens are some of the only known creatures who can see into the ultra-violet.

name: Cow & Bull
description: Domesticated cattle bred for meat or milk production. There are dozens of specialized breeds. Cows have no attribute bonuses. Bulls receive PS +2 and Spd +2. Base stats (size, BIO-E, length, weight) apply to both.

name: Crow
description: These clever scavengers live off human crops. They are very intelligent and social. In North America, there are two common species, the Common Raven and the Fish Crow.

name: Deer
description: Woodland grazing animal whose primary defense against predators is speed. There are a large variety of deer, all with roughly the same characteristics.

name: Dog
description: Because there are so many different breeds, figures given are for the average mongrel dog. Players should roll their exact breed. BIO-E is reduced by 10 for each Size Level above 5. Attribute bonuses vary by Size: SL 3-5 gain IQ +2; SL 6-8 gain IQ +2, ME +1, PS +2, PP +1, PE +2.

name: Duck
description: There are dozens of separate species of aquatic birds including ducks, geese, and swans. Most are migratory and spend most of their time either flying or on the water. Ducks can Hold Breath.

name: Elephant
description: This is the world's largest land animal. The elephant is used as a circus performer throughout the world. It is a useful worker with tremendous strength and a trunk that serves as a flexible (partial) human hand.

name: Elk
description: These large, northern grazing animals are the second largest kind of deer. They are very social animals with the herds moving to the mountains in summer and the valleys in winter.

name: Felines
description: The great felines are basically similar. Includes Jaguars (Size 12, BIO-E 20), Lions (Size 14, BIO-E 10), and Tigers (Size 15, BIO-E 5). Tigers are the largest and solitary hunters; Lions are social hunters. American Jaguars and African Leopards are roughly the same size. Base stats shown are for Jaguar. Tigers receive PS +4 instead of +2.
length: Jaguar: 4.5-6.5 ft; Lion: 4.5-6.5 ft; Tiger: 6-9.5 ft
weight: Jaguar: to 300 lbs; Lion: to 400 lbs; Tiger: to 500 lbs

name: Frog
description: There are hundreds of different kinds of frogs in North America alone. Some are purely aquatic but others have adapted to living on the ground or in trees. Most are insect eaters who rely on their sticky tongue.

name: Goat
description: A domesticated animal used for milk or meat the world over. Goats are valued because of their ability to survive in harsh climates and on relatively little food.

name: Hippopotamus
description: These giant grazing animals spend most of their lives in the water.

name: Horse
description: There are quite a few different breeds of this domesticated grazing animal. Figures given are for typical riding horses.
length: to 60 inches tall at the shoulder

name: Marten & Mink
description: Beautiful, fox-faced animals with gorgeous fur. Great climbers, incurably curious. Fishers and Minks have similar characteristics.

name: Mole
description: This animal is completely adapted to a burrowing existence. Moles can Hold Breath.

name: Monkey
description: There are at least a dozen different kinds of monkeys kept as pets or research animals in the U.S. Although the markings and fur may differ, the animal characteristics are the same. All monkeys are tree dwellers with prehensile tails.

name: Moose
description: These huge animals graze in the swamps and marshes of the northern U.S. and Canada. Moose during rutting season or while guarding young are very dangerous.

name: Mountain Lion
description: Found throughout North America, Mountain Lions have been called Cougars, Panthers, Pumas and other names. While smaller than lions and tigers, they are very agile and bring down deer and other prey much larger than themselves. A typical Mountain Lion will kill a deer every week.

name: Muskrat
description: Muskrats are common throughout North America and Europe. They are river-dwelling vegetarians and scavengers. Although not as talented as Beavers, they do build dens with underwater entrances.

name: Opossum
description: 'Possums look like large, scrawny rats with naked ears, silver-tipped fur and long scaly tails.

name: Otter
description: There are two major varieties of these playful creatures. River Otters (Size 4, BIO-E 50) are found throughout North America. Sea Otters (Size 6, BIO-E 40) are much larger and live on the Pacific coast. Base stats shown are for River Otter.
length: River Otter: 45-55 inches; Sea Otter: 48-53 inches
weight: River Otter: 15-20 lbs; Sea Otter: 45-60 lbs

name: Owl
description: Owls are predatory birds that operate primarily at night. There are quite a few varieties, ranging in size and abilities.

name: Parrot
description: There are over 300 species of parrot, each with a more colorful plumage than the next. Some parrots raised in captivity can be taught to imitate human voices.

name: Pet Birds
description: There are several varieties of tropical songbirds kept as pets. Parakeets and budgies are the most popular.

name: Pig
description: Domesticated pigs can be fattened up to huge proportions. As a meat animal they are very efficient, producing a large amount of food relative to their consumption.

name: Pigeon
description: Pigeons have adapted completely to city life. They are scavengers with a lifestyle that makes them a serious health hazard.

name: Porcupine
description: Porcupines have evolved a natural defense of bristling sharp quills. This makes them invulnerable to most predators and easy prey to Fishers who include them as a regular part of their diet.

name: Rabbit
description: Different kinds of rabbits are found around the world. They are small vegetarians who depend on speed for defense against predators.

name: Raccoon
description: These creatures are very adaptable. They are predominately active at night.

name: Rhinoceros
description: Aggressive grazing animals inhabiting grasslands. They are good runners and can reach up to 35mph. They are also strong enough to overturn a truck. African Black and White Rhinos have two horns while the Asian Great Indian Rhino has just one.

name: Rodent
description: Includes Mice (Size 1, BIO-E 80), Hamsters (Size 1, BIO-E 80), Rats (Size 2, BIO-E 75), and Guinea Pigs (Size 2, BIO-E 75), as well as a wide variety of other small animals. All are good scavengers and spend a lot of time underground. Base stats shown are for Mice/Hamsters.
length: Mice/Hamsters: 6-8 inches; Rats/Guinea Pigs: to 14 inches
weight: Mice/Hamsters: to 1 lb; Rats/Guinea Pigs: to 5 lbs

name: Sheep
description: This domesticated animal has been bred for meat and wool. Sheep are grazing animals found throughout the world.

name: Skunk
description: Most North American Skunks are of the Striped (Size 3, BIO-E 70) or Spotted (Size 2, BIO-E 80) variety. These animals use their skunk glands as a defense against predators. Base stats shown are for Striped Skunk.

name: Sparrow
description: The common House Sparrow is actually related to finches. This bird was imported from Europe and has become commonplace in urban and suburban areas.

name: Squirrel
description: This is the most common wild mammal in urban areas. They live in the trees and have tremendous tree climbing abilities. Chipmunks and ground squirrels are similar but lack climbing claws. Flying Squirrels are the only kind that can glide.

name: Turkey
description: The domestic turkey is a flightless, running bird that has been domesticated as a food animal. Wild turkeys are much leaner and faster.

name: Turtle
description: Turtles live in a wide variety of environments. Some turtles are purely aquatic, others have adapted to deserts. The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles were common, small pond turtles.

name: Weasel
description: Both short-tailed and long-tailed weasels are fearless carnivores. Their lifestyle is based on constant killing of whatever prey is available, even animals much larger than themselves. They are long-bodied animals so narrow that they can fit into very small holes.

name: Wild Birds
description: Wild birds are the common seed and insect eaters in North American forests. Included are Blue Jays, Cardinals, Robins and a host of other small birds.

name: Wild Fowl
description: These are birds that spend most of their time on the ground hunting seeds and insects. Also known as game birds because of their popularity with hunters. They tend to be heavier and poorer flyers than other wild birds. Included are Grouse, Partridge, Pheasant, and Quail.

name: Wild Predatory Birds
description: All these predatory birds share curved beaks (for ripping prey), sharp talons, keen eyesight and the ability to dive directly onto their prey. Included are eagles, falcons and hawks.
weight: Hawks/Falcons: 8-15 lbs; Eagles: up to 25 lbs

name: Wolverine
description: These northern carnivores have incredible endurance and can travel 20 or more miles a day through heavy snow in pursuit of prey. They are tough enough to kill animals many times their own size and will even drive away full grown bears. The fur does not collect ice and they are greatly resistant to cold. Wolverine characters can Jump or Leap an extra 5 feet.
